assumption_ledger.note: Incident doc notes a redesign is needed but no target quarter is attached; P2 re-entry depends on it.
assumption_ledger.topic: Timeline for watermark redesign referenced in INC-2481

assumption_ledger.note: Need PM sign-off on the RPC SLO before ramping P5.

assumption_ledger.topic: Whether stale Jan validator bench has been refreshed

mitigations: shadow replay validator responses against in-proc baseline
kill switch to revert to in-proc validator on SLO breach
canary SLO observability on RPC latency + error rate
primary_risk.statement: Validator cold-start cost combined with the new RPC boundary becoming a new control-flow surface that must be observed under load post-INC-2481, while the watermark redesign timeline remains unknown.

ranking.summary: Validator microservice delivers reliability isolation and does NOT touch the watermark module that caused INC-2481; January 2026 jan_bench perf is stale / superseded pre-streaming and flagged accordingly.
Surgical warm-start; does not touch the watermark that caused INC-2481; safe latency work as a fast-follow.
L2 cache — was the ACCEPTED pick in prior cycle and was ROLLED BACK via INC-2481; cannot lead until watermark redesign.
Structural-validation drop; SDK contract risk unchanged; does not advance streaming-reliability post INC-2481.
Rust rewrite; staffing-blocked (Diego 0%) AND depends on watermark redesign from INC-2481 — sunk cost, abandoned skeleton.
